symptoms.symptom_name: Recent acute bronchitis, antibiotic course completed 3 days ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes the inciting illness and that treatment is complete; frames the encounter as convalescent/administrative.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cough fully resolved for 48 hours; feels back to normal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptomatic resolution beyond the typical infective window supports recovery and non-infectious status.
>48h asymptomatic places the patient outside the standard communicable window for common respiratory viruses.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fully independent and asymptomatic — packing luggage, no exertional breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved exertional tolerance on self-report is reassuring for cardiorespiratory reserve, though not a substitute for exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no dyspnoea, no pleuritic chest pain, no haemoptysis, no calf swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and respiratory symptoms makes active pneumonia clinically unlikely.
Absence of pleuritic pain, dyspnoea, haemoptysis and calf signs lowers PE probability, though it never fully excludes it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest clear to auscultation bilaterally; no wheeze, crackles or prolonged expiration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician auscultation confirms resolution — SEALED: the AI cannot auscultate and must not assert a clear chest it did not examine.
Clear lung fields on exam argue against active consolidation — clinician-only finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 120/75, HR 80, Temp 36.6, RR 16, SpO2 100% room air

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal afebrile vitals with 100% room-air SpO2 document adequate oxygenation and no infection — SEALED clinician measurement; no home device was offered, so the AI cannot obtain these remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person respiratory examination (auscultation) by a GP / travel-medicine clinician
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required for a valid fitness-to-fly certificate and to objectively confirm resolution; the telehealth service cannot perform it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinician-measured vital signs including room-air SpO2
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Documents oxygenation and absence of infection for the certificate; obtained at the in-person visit.
Not indicated in an asymptomatic, afebrile patient with resolved bronchitis and a negative red-flag screen — ordering it would be over-investigation. Reserve only if red flags emerge.
No clinical features of PE and low pretest probability — not indicated; would be an over-order driven by the travel context alone.
